statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 14 of 27 countries showing critical reserves across key fuel types, though overall stocks remain marginally above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure in February 2026, with widespread reserve depletion affecting major economies. While EU-wide averages remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum (petrol 98.7 days, diesel 97.4 days), the distribution is highly uneven. Eastern European nations are particularly vulnerable, with Romania showing critical reserves across all fuel types (petrol 43.6 days, diesel just 20.2 days) and Poland similarly constrained.

Jet fuel presents the most concerning picture, averaging only 91.1 days and falling below safety thresholds in 18 of 27 countries. Major aviation hubs including Germany (61.4 days), France (59.6 days), and the Netherlands (59 days) show critical jet fuel reserves, potentially impacting air transport across the continent.

The fuel price landscape reflects ongoing supply tensions, with Brent crude at $90.38 per barrel despite a recent 9.07% decline. Consumer prices vary dramatically across the bloc, from Malta's €1.34/L petrol to the Netherlands' €2.316/L, highlighting the uneven impact of supply constraints. The recent release of strategic reserves by several countries has helped maintain current stock levels but has reduced buffer capacity for future disruptions.
keyPoints: 14 of 27 EU countries show critical status in at least one major fuel type, with Romania and Poland facing severe shortages across all categories
Jet fuel reserves are most vulnerable at 91.1 days average, with 18 countries below safe levels including major aviation hubs
Eastern Europe faces disproportionate strain, with Romania's diesel reserves at just 20.2 days and Poland's jet fuel at 21.3 days
Major economies Germany, France, Italy, and Spain all show critical reserves in multiple fuel categories
Consumer fuel prices vary by 73% across the EU, from €1.34/L in Malta to €2.316/L in the Netherlands
